title: Invalidate Stack Elements: Miscounted Elements in Dynamic Array

statement.markdown: Given a sequence of push and pop operations, validate whether the stack elements satisfy the condition. If at any point the number of pop operations exceeds the number of push operations or the stack is not empty at the end, return False. Otherwise, return True.
statement.inputFormat: A sequence of integers, where 1 represents a push operation and -1 represents a pop operation
statement.outputFormat: A boolean value indicating whether the stack elements are valid
statement.notes: Note that the sequence of operations may contain multiple push and pop operations. The stack initially has no elements.

hints.text: Analyze the push and pop operations separately
Use a dynamic array to simulate the stack

starterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: bool validateStackSequences(vector<int>& pushed, vector<int>& popped); };
starterCode.java: public class Solution { public boolean validateStackSequences(int[] pushed, int[] popped); }
starterCode.python: class Solution: def validateStackSequences(self, pushed: list[int], popped: list[int]) -> bool:

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: bool validateStackSequences(vector<int>& pushed, vector<int>& popped) { int len = pushed.size(); int j = len - 1; stack<int> st; for (int i = 0; i < len; i++) { if (pushed[i] == -1) { while (!st.empty() && !popped[i] == -st.top()) st.pop(); if (st.top() == popped[i]) st.pop(); } else { st.push(pushed[i]); } } return st.empty() && i == j; } };
buggyStarterCode.java: public class Solution { public boolean validateStackSequences(int[] pushed, int[] popped) { int len = pushed.length; int j = len - 1; for (int i = 0; i < len; i++) { if (pushed[i] == -1) { while (!stack.empty() && !popped[i] == -(stack.peek())) stack.pop(); if (stack.peek() == popped[i]) stack.pop(); } else { stack.push(pushed[i]); } } return stack.empty() && i == j; } }
buggyStarterCode.python: class Solution: def validateStackSequences(self, pushed: list[int], popped: list[int]) -> bool: len_pushed = len(pushed); j = len_pushed - 1; st = []; for i in range(len_pushed): if pushed[i] == -1: while not st and popped[i] != -st[-1]: st.pop(); if st[-1] == popped[i]: st.pop(); else: st.append(pushed[i]); return not st and i == j;
buggyStarterCode.javascript: function validateStackSequences(pushed, popped) { var len_pushed = pushed.length; var j = len_pushed - 1; var st = []; for (var i = 0; i < len_pushed; i++) { if (pushed[i] == -1) { while (!st.length && popped[i] != -st[st.length - 1]) st.pop(); if (st[st.length - 1] == popped[i]) st.pop(); else { st.push(pushed[i]); } } } return !st.length && i == j; }

functionSignature.python: def validateStackSequences(self, pushed: list[int], popped: list[int]) -> bool;
